hook_check.probe: invoke each resolver from a clone whose root basename != '.claude'; assert printed path == clone root and exit 0
set CLAUDE_HOME=/tmp/wrong; assert resolver still returns script-walk root; then set CLAUDE_HOME=<symlink to real root>; assert honored
call resolve_required on a missing relpath in both helpers; assert exit code == 2 and a block reason is on stderr
call resolve_optional on a missing relpath; assert it signals absent without exiting the process non-2

(a) simulate import failure of the deny module post-migration -> assert exit == 2 AND NOT 0 AND NOT 1, block marker on stderr; (b) relocate the policy file and request a dev-role Write -> assert deny (exit 2); (c) unparseable policy JSON -> assert exit 2
(a) verifier absent + spec touched -> assert exit 2 (not 0); (b) verifier present + under-covered split -> assert exit 2
feed a payload with agent_id set + an unresolvable role + a protected Write target with no grace sentinel; assert exit == 2 (not 0)
set HOME=/home/alice with a protected path under it; assert the ~/relative variant is generated and the guard protects it; assert no /root-only branch is required
unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and HOME; invoke each site from a non-root clone; assert no resolved path contains the literal '/root' as an author-home default; security-relevant sites fail closed
render against a temp home; grep executable fields for forbidden literals -> zero hits; assert GRAPHIFY_BIN reflects the temp home
feed a template that renders to invalid JSON / drops a required hook -> assert renderer exits nonzero and live settings.json is byte-identical to before
diff rendered permissions vs template (placeholders normalized) -> only placeholder substitutions differ; no new/broadened allow entries

plant /root in a copy of settings.json -> detector flags it; plant a bare /root default in a hook (PROJ="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-/root}", os.environ.get("HOME","/root")) -> detector flags each; plant a JSON "default": "/root" -> detector flags it; plant a Write(file_path="/root/...") AND a Read(file_path="/root/...") tool-operand literal in a command .md -> detector flags both; plant a /root/docs/... plain-prose dispatch-read ('Read /root/docs/foo.md' / 'Follow instructions in /root/...') in a command .md -> detector flags it; plant a CONTROL_ROOT=/root runtime default in an agent .md -> detector flags it; clean rendered file + a per-literal-allowlisted prose mention -> detector passes

structurally compare permissions.{deny,ask,allow} and hooks.* before/after render: assert deny set is preserved (no drop), ask preserved, allow not broadened, hook arrays identical in entries+order; compute the human-only Skill deny set from the command/skill inventory and assert all present

unset the helper env var + remove /root/bin/*; run a --codex flow -> assert it does NOT invoke bare codex and either fails closed or prints 'unavailable'; set CODEX_ISO_BIN to a temp wrapper -> assert it is used
create a gitignored file in a synced dir, run the generator, assert the gitignored file is absent from the output
run generator on a representative dir; grep output for 'unknown file' and empty-description list items -> zero
extract generated count + curated badge count; assert equal

from a fresh non-root clone with /root absent, run a full-surface scan over commands/*.md + agents/*.md + skills/*/SKILL.md + schemas/*.json for author-absolute path literals in the four load-bearing classes (a/b/c/d) USING the boundary-aware (?<![\w./])(?:/root(?:/|(?=$|[^\w./-]))|/dev/shm/dev-workspace(?:/|(?=$|[^\w./-]))) pattern (catches /root/docs + /root/templates + bare-/root env-default boundary forms); assert ZERO remain (allowlisting only purely-illustrative prose by per-literal entry); assert each evidence anchor is fixed: dev-overnight.md:256-258 uses the Bash-redirect $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR form or an orchestrator-inlined resolved absolute path (NOT a literal /root and NOT an unexpanded shell-var token inside the Write operand); commit.md:341/343 dispatch-read resolves via the resolver; qa.md:896/905 uses UI_EVIDENCE_AUDIT_BIN; changelog-analyst.md:17 default is resolver/env-derived not /root; dev-command.md:68 dispatch-read resolves via the resolver/$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR; ui-specialist.md:10 + dev-overnight.md:1882 template ref resolves portably; commit.md:50 fallback-default tracks the migrated resolve-close-report.sh; assert per-literal-allowlisted prose lines (dev.md:1566/1570/1592/1598/1612/1618) are unchanged

grep commands/spec.md COVERAGE step; assert it no longer contains a 'skip ... manual coverage check' fail-open branch and instead routes a missing required verifier to a block/install-error path resolved via the resolver

from a fresh non-root clone, exercise a skill read path + a schema-default consumer; assert the data file resolves within the clone (no /root dependency) OR a per-literal non-load-bearing proof is recorded
run session-start + a /dev-shaped step with all optional capabilities absent; assert no crash/hang, exit 0, and a one-line unavailable message present
grep README for the contract wording: a 'core runs / extras need setup' separation + a reference to the fresh-clone smoke test
grep for a trust-model statement and a fail-closed-vs-skip table with both 'fail closed' and 'skip' rows

run bootstrap against a temp home -> assert venv created, manifest deps installed, resolver self-check passes, exit 0; run against a pre-existing home without opt-in -> assert it refuses to clobber and prints restore guidance
uninstall a required dep -> doctor names it + exits nonzero; install all -> doctor exits 0
parse the manifest; assert pytest, jsonschema, pyyaml present
run install over a populated home without opt-in -> refuse + restore doc; with opt-in -> backup created before apply
run the smoke test from a temp HOME not under /root or /dev/shm; assert each guard's negative case returns exit 2 with its distinctive marker (distinguishable from an ordinary permission prompt)
plant a canary that only the intended guard would act on; run each guard in a fresh env -i subprocess; assert the observed block reason references the canary / intended guard
assert exit-code matrix (allowed=0, blocked=2, none=1/126/127 for required) and clean stderr

(a) feed an under-covered spec split -> assert exit 2; (b) hide the required verifier + touch a spec -> assert exit 2 with install-error message (not 0); (c) restore verifier + full coverage -> assert exit 0
run optional-capability + external-helper path with env vars unset; assert one-line unavailable + no crash + no bare-tool fallback; assert test naming/docstring scopes green to core+guards

run the AC-WS3-4 detector (boundary-aware (?<![\w./])(?:/root(?:/|(?=$|[^\w./-]))|/dev/shm/dev-workspace(?:/|(?=$|[^\w./-]))) pattern, four-class context classification) across the rendered fresh clone as an integration gate; assert zero load-bearing author literals in the defined surfaces incl. the /root/docs + /root/templates class AND the bare-/root env-default boundary forms; assert per-literal-allowlisted contexts exempt
run the demo script under a non-root home twice; assert deterministic block-then-grant-then-complete; assert the cast is optional
